notes: GlobalAI purchased 438 acres (former Carestream/Kodak site) at 2000 Howard Smith Avenue West near Windsor for $15.6M in Nov 2025. Plans for 18-24MW initial phase, expandable to 50-60MW and eventually up to 1GW. Partnership with Nvidia and Saudi company HUMAIN. Investment estimated $2B-$20B depending on final scale. Official Nvidia Preferred Cloud Partner using GB200 & GB300 Blackwell clusters.

Dryden banned data centers partly in response to neighboring Lansing's TeraWulf project at the former Cayuga coal plant. TeraWulf (via Cayuga Operating Company) secured 80-year lease on 183 acres for up to 400MW data center. $3.7B in contracted revenue. Google backstops Fluidstack lease obligations and receives ~8% equity stake in TeraWulf. Core42 (G42/Abu Dhabi subsidiary) also leasing 72.5MW. A separate developer had also sent an application directly to Dryden for a data center on George Road near State Route 13, which was denied by planning director Ray Burger.

Bristolville 25 Developer LLC (New York, NY) proposed a $3.6B, 1.65M sq ft data center campus on 133 acres near Tod Avenue spanning Lordstown and Jackson Township in Trumbull/Mahoning counties. Regional data center interest prompted Hubbard Township's moratorium. In Hubbard itself, discussions between City of Hubbard, Lake to River Economic Development, and an unnamed company were underway about a potential technology project near Deer Creek Golf Course. Bristolville 25 filed Ohio Supreme Court complaint after Lordstown banned data centers.
